ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for a mixed gift box of fruit cheese tarts and dacquoises. FOODON_03315091 (pastry food product) used as the closest parent class. Dacquoise is a French meringue-almond layer cake; fruit cheese tart is a shortcrust or pâte sucrée shell filled with cream cheese and fresh fruit.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (Pastries and cakes) is the closest defensible category for this mixed dessert gift box. No specific code exists for dacquoise or fruit cheese tart combinations in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT clinical concept maps to this specific dessert gift box product.

serving.description: 1 gift box containing 2 fruit cheese tarts and 3 dacquoises
serving.natural_language_portion: One gift box (approximately 500 g) contains 2 premium fruit cheese tarts and 3 dacquoises, providing roughly 2000 kcal per box — intended for sharing among 3–5 people as a dessert or gift occasion.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Cheesecake (SR Legacy) and Dacquoise / Meringue Cake components; Singapore HPB Nutrient Composition of Food — Tart and Pastry reference values
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service; Health Promotion Board, Singapore
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single approved-source entry exists for a combined fruit cheese tart and dacquoise gift box. Nutrient values are estimated by weighted composition: 2 fruit cheese tarts (~120 g each, ~240 g total) and 3 dacquoises (~87 g each, ~260 g total) totalling ~500 g. Cheese tart macros anchored to USDA FoodData Central 'Cheesecake' (FDC ID 167574) and HPB Singapore tart reference. Dacquoise macros anchored to USDA FoodData Central 'Meringue cookie' (FDC ID 168917) and 'Almond cake' (FDC ID 172757) blended estimate. Input values (energy 400 kcal, protein 6 g, fat 28 g, carbohydrate 32 g, sugar 20 g, sodium 160 mg, fibre 1 g, saturated fat 16 g, trans fat 0.3 g, cholesterol 80 mg, calcium 40 mg, iron 0.6 mg, vitamin C 6 mg per 100 g) are consistent with this blended estimate and are retained with medium confidence.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Cheesecake GI ~46; Meringue/almond cake GI ~54–72; blended estimate ~65 for this mixed dessert.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific gift box product. GI estimated as a weighted blend: fruit cheese tart (analogous to cheesecake, GI ~46, University of Sydney GI Database) and dacquoise (analogous to almond meringue cake, GI ~72–80 due to high sugar and meringue content, per Atkinson et al. 2008). Weighted blend at approximately 48% tart / 52% dacquoise by mass yields blended GI ~65 (medium). Glycemic load per 500 g serving = (65 × 32 g carbohydrate per 100 g × 500 g / 100) / 100 = 104 g GL units. This is a high glycemic load per full box serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Saturated fat 16 g/100 g and sugar 20 g/100 g with fibre only 1 g/100 g
hypertension.guidance: Saturated fat at 16 g/100 g far exceeds the 5 g/100 g threshold and promotes arterial stiffness; sugar at 20 g/100 g with fibre of only 1 g/100 g drives insulin-mediated sodium retention — individuals with hypertension should avoid this product or limit intake to a single small piece (≤30 g) on rare occasions.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 20 g/100 g, carbohydrate 32 g/100 g, dietary fibre only 1 g/100 g, GI ~65 (medium-high blended)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The combination of 20 g sugar per 100 g, 32 g total carbohydrate, minimal fibre (1 g/100 g), and a blended GI of ~65 produces a high glycemic load per serving (GL ~104 per box); individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this product entirely or restrict intake to a single tart or dacquoise piece (≤50 g) with close post-meal glucose monitoring.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 16 g/100 g, trans fat 0.3 g/100 g, cholesterol 80 mg/100 g
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Saturated fat at 16 g/100 g (80% of daily limit per 100 g), trans fat at 0.3 g/100 g, and cholesterol at 80 mg/100 g collectively raise LDL cholesterol and cardiovascular risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat this product as an occasional indulgence only, limiting intake to ≤50 g per occasion and avoiding regular consumption.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Saturated fat 16 g/100 g and sugar 20 g/100 g are strongly pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Saturated fat at 16 g/100 g promotes TNF-alpha and IL-6 inflammatory cytokine activity; sugar at 20 g/100 g exceeds the 15 g/100 g pro-inflammatory threshold — individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this product or limit intake to very small portions on rare occasions.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Deluxe Gift Box is a premium Singaporean patisserie gift set containing 2 fruit cheese tarts and 3 dacquoises, assembled for gifting or sharing occasions. The fruit cheese tarts feature a buttery shortcrust or pâte sucrée pastry shell filled with a smooth, lightly sweetened cream cheese filling and topped with fresh seasonal fruit such as strawberries, blueberries, or kiwi slices. The dacquoises are French-inspired layered confections made from crisp almond-meringue discs sandwiched with buttercream or ganache, offering a contrast of crunchy exterior and soft, moist interior. Both components are baked and then assembled chilled. The overall flavour profile is rich, buttery, and sweet, with fruity brightness from the tart toppings. Nutritionally, this gift box is energy-dense at approximately 400 kcal per 100 g, with high fat (28 g/100 g, of which 16 g is saturated fat), moderate-to-high carbohydrate (32 g/100 g, including 20 g sugar), and low dietary fibre (1 g/100 g). The high saturated fat, sugar, and trans fat content make this product unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is best consumed as an occasional treat in small portions. The full 500 g box delivers approximately 2000 kcal and a glycemic load of ~104, making portion control essential for metabolically vulnerable individuals.

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for this specific combined gift box product. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA FoodData Central cheesecake and meringue/almond cake reference entries, cross-checked against Singapore HPB pastry reference values. The input values from the GrabFood listing are consistent with this blended estimate and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2008. Medium confidence is appropriate given the composite nature of the product and the absence of a direct laboratory analysis.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite dessert gift box is not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: micronutrient and moisture data for this composite product are not available in USDA FoodData Central or Singapore HPB database as of 2024. trans_fat_g is 0.3 g/100 g as reported in the input record, consistent with butter-containing pastry products. All core macros (protein_g 6.0, fat_g 28.0, carbohydrate_g 32.0) are non-zero and nutritionally plausible for a cream cheese and almond meringue dessert. Halal status is unconfirmed as the product contains gelatin-risk ingredients (cream, butter) from unspecified sources; is_halal set to false pending certification confirmation. The input source URL (https://foodo.sg/) was not used as a primary nutrient source; USDA FoodData Central and HPB Singapore were used instead per approved source policy. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 to comply with allowed values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] per G5 validation rules.
